other_observers: Jeff Dunning; I am the original finder of the bird.
duration: Twenty seconds visual. Thirty minutes singing.
specific_location: Approximately 3.7-4.0 miles in on the trail to Black Lake in Rocky Mountain National Park (Glacier Gorge Trailhead). The bird was located in one of the most-jumbled areas of the large blowdown that occurred in that valley during the prior winter.
description: A very small bird, evenly dark brown with rusty/orange tones (the light was very harsh), it had a dark needle-like bill and the itty-bitty stub-tail which it pointed straight up.Behaviors: Almost impossible to see visually as it was buried in the jumble of the blowdown. I only got the brief view of it described above.  Singing the same song over and over and over again for the entire timeframe I was present.Call: Attached audio clipsPlumage: Adult
